Aaron Cometbus Punk and Underground Press Collection, ca. 1977-2018.
Collection Number: 8107

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
Aaron Cometbus Punk and Underground Press Collection, ca. 1977-2018.
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
8107
Abstract:
Flyers, posters, magazines, files, and zines relating to American punk and alternative culture collected or created by Aaron Cometbus, including his own work as a writer and musician.
Creator:
Cometbus, Aaron, 1968-
Quanitities:
14.9 cubic feet.
Language:
Collection material in English
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE

Aaron Elliott, better known as Aaron Cometbus, has published Cometbus magazine since 1981. He is also an accomplished novelist, lyricist, and drummer.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION

Flyers, posters, magazines, files, and zines relating to American punk and alternative culture collected or created by Aaron Cometbus, including his own work as a writer and musician. The archive contains hundreds of fanzines and small magazines, plus original manuscripts and paste-ups for Cometbus and related publishing and distribution records. Board games, business cards, set lists, and venue histories provide a personal, behind-the-scenes view of punk both as music and as a movement, from 1980 to the present.
NOTES

Introduction to the collection written by Aaron Elliot can be found in Box 1, Folder A.
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Cite As:

Aaron Cometbus Punk and Underground Press Collection, #8107. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Conditions Governing Use

Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections webpage.
Conditions Governing Use

Audio visual media that has been digitized: TR-15991.

CONTAINER LIST
Container
Description
Date
Box 1 Folder a
Introductory essay
 2016
Box 1 Folder a
"Anarchy in the Archives" edition of Cometbus
 2016
Series I. Punk and Underground Press Materials
 1963-2016
Scope and Contents
Includes fanzines, magazines, newsletters, comics, and newspapers
The first two subseries are arranged geographically starting with locations in the Bay Area and then alphabetically by title within each location.
Processing Information
"Creator" is used throughout Series I to denote the author, editor, or publisher of a work.
Bay Area
 1977-2016
Processing Information
Folders previously numbered 4-100 and 4-101 have been reprocessed and are now numbered 17-9 and 17-10.

Box 12 Folder 13
Epi-Logue - The Zine of Epicenter Zone's Demise
 1995
creator: Zola, Gordon
San Francisco, California
Box 17 Folder 32
Famous Hardcore of Punkland
 1985
Scope and Contents
Two issues. Donor note: This fanzine shouldn't be taken as a representative example of the form, the scene, or the time. "Punkette" was an alien phrase then as now, and what made this mag interesting was how totally off the editor seemed, perhaps a 45-year old shut-in reporting on a youth culture that excited him but which he knew little about. Sort of a precursor to the internet!

Scope and Contents
Donor note: A S.F. free-love-and-computers cult rebranded their journal as an alt. rock mag briefly, before their break up. The result is strange indeed: mostly a failed recruiting tool, but with a disarming Joey Ramone interview in this issue.

Posters
 1982-1999
Box 8 Folder 8
Advertisement for "Hardcore Storms America"
 Undated
Scope and Contents
U.K. Subs, Anti-Nowhere League. Note from donor: This caused a huge laugh. Of course, hardcore was particularly and uniquely American at that point, and certainly wasn't represented by two geezer/biker UK bands, even if the two bands happened to be pretty good.

Series VII. Sound Recordings
 1982-2009
Box 14 SR-20751-20758
7" Vinyl Records
 1988-2008
Scope and Contents
Contains: "Songs of Wrath and Optimism" by Astrid Oto, "Untitled" by Astrid Oto, "House Band Feud" by The Blank Fight, "Untitled" by Cleveland Bound Death Sentence, "Sleep, What's That? EP" by Crimpshrine, "On the Waterfront EP" by Crybaby MacArthur, "Untitled" by Pinhead Gunpowder, and "Redmond Shooting Stars" by Redmond Shooting Stars.
Box 15 TR-15977-15993
Cassettes
 1982-2009
Scope and Contents
Contains an introduction by Aaron Cometbus and the cassette tapes: "Banned in R.C." by Blatz, "Rokken' Like Dokken'" by The Blottos, "Live at the Metropolis" by Brap Smears, "Brent's T.V." by Brent's T.V., "Through Our Eyes" by Complete Disorder, "Untitled" by Crimpshrine, "Driller Killers" by Driller Killers, "Arbgabdo Baby!" by Fleshies, "Eartraining for Corporates" by Harbinger, "Jüke @ Gilman" by Jüke, "Untitled" by Maniax, "Bloody Marys…" by Premarital Sex, "It's Not Just a Band…It's a Lifestyle" by Soup, and "Pug's Not Dead" by Zombie Dogs. Also contains the compilation tapes "Gimme Cooties," "Absolutely Zippo Presents...'Time Capsule'," "Every Dog Has its Blues: The Bands of Lucky Dog," and "We Got Glue."
